name: Filtering & Sorting
description: How filtering and sorting controls should help users efficiently narrow and organise product results

heuristics.principle: Category-relevant filters should be immediately visible without requiring interaction to reveal them
checkpoints: Filter panel or bar is visible on initial page load
Filters are not hidden behind a collapsed menu on desktop
Most relevant filters appear first (e.g. size, colour, price for apparel)
Filter options show available count per value where practical

heuristics.principle: Applied filters should be clearly indicated with an easy way to remove individual selections or clear all
checkpoints: Active filters are displayed as removable tags or chips
Each filter can be individually deselected
A clear all filters option is available when filters are active
Product count updates to reflect current filter state

heuristics.principle: Sorting options should include the most useful criteria and clearly indicate the current sort order
checkpoints: Sort dropdown or control is visible near the top of results
Options include relevance, price low-high, price high-low, and newest
Currently active sort order is clearly indicated
Changing sort order does not reset applied filters

heuristics.principle: Price filter should support range selection rather than only predefined brackets
checkpoints: Price filter allows minimum and maximum value input
Slider or input fields are provided for price range
Price range reflects the actual range of available products
Results update to show only products within the selected range

heuristics.principle: Filter interactions should not cause full page reloads or disorient the user
checkpoints: Applying a filter updates results without full page reload
Scroll position is maintained or reset to the top of results
Loading indicator is shown during filter application
URL updates to reflect filter state for shareability
